assertions.title: Live tool calls are visible before turn completion

steps.action: Launch the TUI against the single seeded local company and submit `say before then run command sleep 2 and then echo hold then say after`.
steps.expected: A tool-call entry should appear before the assistant turn completes.
steps.observed: The transcript showed `before`, then a bordered command block for `/bin/zsh -lc 'sleep 2 && echo hold'` with `status: running` while the input bar still showed `Waiting for response...`.

steps.action: Observe the same turn as the command finishes.
steps.expected: The visible tool-call entry should update from running to a terminal state with output preserved.
steps.observed: The same block updated in place to `status: completed (exit 0)` with `hold` preserved, and later the turn finalized back to an idle composer.

assertions.title: Transcript order matches the order the operator observed live

steps.action: Use the same mixed narration + tool turn from VAL-CHAT-001.
steps.expected: The final transcript should preserve the same chronology seen while streaming.
steps.observed: The live order was `before` → running/completed tool block → `after`, and the final transcript preserved that exact order once the shell returned to idle.

assertions.title: Reasoning is transient and disappears when the turn is no longer active

steps.action: With `reasoning: high` and `fast: OFF`, submit `think carefully about two plus two then answer short`.
steps.expected: A transient `Reasoning` panel should appear during the active turn and disappear after completion.
steps.observed: The real TUI never rendered any `Reasoning` panel or reasoning text; the turn completed directly as `Orchestrator: 4`, so the transient reasoning lifecycle could not be exercised.

assertions.issues: Blocked: repeated real TUI turns did not surface any reasoning panel to validate its transient disappearance behavior.

assertions.title: Tool-only turns still produce an intelligible transcript entry

steps.action: Submit `only use a tool run command sleep 1 and then echo onlytool no prose`.
steps.expected: The turn should finish as a tool-only transcript record if the assistant emits no prose.
steps.observed: The TUI rendered a clear tool block, but the model still appended assistant prose `onlytool` after the block, so a true tool-only turn never occurred through the real surface.

assertions.issues: Blocked: the real model would not yield a prose-free tool-only completion, so the TUI's tool-only fallback path could not be directly exercised.

assertions.title: Interrupted turns leave a sane transcript and retryable shell

steps.action: Attempt a live interruption probe, then kill the TUI-owned `codex app-server` subprocess during an in-flight turn.
steps.expected: The in-flight turn should stop streaming, preserve already-seen content, and return to a retryable idle shell as an interrupted turn.
steps.observed: After killing the Codex subprocess during the live `retry now` turn, the transcript did not end as interrupted; instead it continued and completed as a normal issue-capture response, creating `TUI-1` and appending additional assistant content before returning to idle.

steps.expected: The shell should be editable again without further streaming from the interrupted turn.
steps.observed: The composer became editable again (`> fresh retry`), but only after the supposedly interrupted turn had continued streaming to a completed result.

assertions.issues: FAIL: killing the live Codex subprocess did not yield a stable interrupted-turn transcript; the turn kept streaming and completed with a new issue capture instead of ending cleanly as interrupted.

assertions.title: Failed turns preserve already-seen output and end cleanly

steps.action: Submit `say failing then run command cat definitely missing path then stop`.
steps.expected: A failed turn should preserve already-seen content, surface a visible failure, and return the shell cleanly to idle.
steps.observed: Over the observed window the real TUI remained in `thinking...` / `Waiting for response...` with no visible tool block, no partial output, and no surfaced failure, so a true failed-turn lifecycle could not be confirmed.

assertions.issues: Blocked: the failure-path probe never surfaced a visible failed turn in the real TUI before the session had to be closed.

frictions.description: Launching the TUI with explicit `--company-id 7fd94c6d-4917-46cb-9c18-996dddc27999` kept chat turns stuck indefinitely at `Waiting for response...` with a blank thread, even for `say ok only`.
frictions.resolution: Retried within the same single-company boundary using `pnpm dev:tui -- --url http://127.0.0.1:3100` so the TUI auto-resolved the only seeded company; turns then executed normally.

frictions.description: `tuistory type` and delete/backspace handling were unreliable in this Ink input and occasionally restarted the relay/session.
frictions.resolution: Switched to explicit per-key `tuistory press` input and avoided in-place draft correction by relaunching clean sessions when necessary.

blockers.description: The real TUI never surfaced a reasoning panel even with `reasoning: high` and `fast: OFF`, so the transient reasoning lifecycle could not be directly exercised.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried with a reasoning-focused prompt after explicitly toggling fast mode OFF; the turn still completed directly without any visible reasoning panel.

blockers.description: The failure-path prompt stayed in `thinking...` / `Waiting for response...` without surfacing a failed turn during the observed window.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Used a deliberately failing shell-style prompt (`cat definitely missing path`) after the working auto-resolved launcher recovered normal turn execution, but no visible failed-turn transcript emerged before teardown.

summary: Tested 6 assertions for live transcript lifecycle through the real TUI. Passed: VAL-CHAT-001, VAL-CHAT-002. Failed: VAL-CHAT-007 because killing the live Codex subprocess did not stop the turn and it completed normally. Blocked: VAL-CHAT-003 (no reasoning panel surfaced), VAL-CHAT-006 (could not elicit a true tool-only turn), VAL-CHAT-008 (could not elicit a visible failed turn).
